resources.string: The sweet meat stinks softly; the mouth opens again.
SWEET MEAT STINKS. THE MOUTH OPENS AGAIN.

The feast smells of perfume, rot, spice, and vomit. Amethyst liquor runs like liquid light, and the diners keep swallowing after their bellies split. Dignity is gone. Speech is gone. Even the tongue is chewed away in search of a purer taste. Fullness is an insult, a locked door in the body. Bite through it. Drink past it. Let appetite become the only organ left.
PERFUME. ROT. SPICE. VOMIT. BELLIES SPLIT. TONGUES ARE CHEWED AWAY. FULLNESS IS AN INSULT. APPETITE REMAINS.
